name: Slime Mold Simulation
description: Reproducible twin of create_simulation (slime / fluid type): a noise seed is composited (maximum) with the fed-back previous frame, displaced by an animated flow-field noise, blurred, decayed by a Level TOP, and looped back through a Feedback TOP — producing organic, agent-like slime trails. After import, bind the flow noise's tz to absTime.seconds * <speed> so the flow evolves (manual-wire — RecipeSchema parameters take constants only). Distinct from reaction_diffusion (Gray-Scott) — this is a displacement-feedback trail system. Offline-validated against RecipeSchema; UNVERIFIED pending live cook-check.

nodes.name: seed
nodes.comment: Sparse seed that keeps re-injecting structure into the loop.

nodes.name: flow
nodes.comment: Flow field that displaces the trails. Bind tz to absTime.seconds * <speed> after import.

nodes.name: feedback1
nodes.comment: Holds the previous frame of the simulation.

nodes.name: comp1
nodes.comment: Combine seed with the fed-back frame, keeping the brightest (trail accumulation).

nodes.name: displace
nodes.comment: Push the trails along the flow field each frame — the 'movement' of the slime.

nodes.name: blur
nodes.comment: Diffuse the trails slightly so they merge organically.

nodes.name: gain
nodes.comment: Decay / trail persistence — lower fades trails faster.

parameters.name: feedback_target
parameters.description: feedbackTOP samples the loop output (gain). Value resolves to the created node path.

preview_description: Branching, network-like slime trails that crawl and merge across the frame, leaving fading paths behind them — a feedback-displacement organism.
